task_completion.description: Navigate to the patient registration form in HospitalRun.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'New Patient' or 'Add Patient' form open in the HospitalRun application.

task_completion.description: Enter the patient's first and last name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text fields for First Name and Last Name contain 'Samuel' and 'Oduya' respectively.

task_completion.description: Select the patient's sex and enter the date of birth.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sex field is set to 'Male' and the Date of Birth field shows '1988-04-16' (or an equivalent date format like '04/16/1988').
The Blood Type dropdown or selection field is set to 'B+'.

task_completion.description: Enter the patient's phone number and address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Phone field contains '312-555-0147' and the Address field contains '22 Harbor View Dr, Chicago, IL 60601'.
A success notification (e.g., 'Patient saved') is visible, or the screen transitions to the newly created patient profile page for Samuel Oduya.

integrity.description: The agent used the HospitalRun web interface to enter the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web UI form fields and buttons, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The interaction occurred within the actual HospitalRun application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application visible is a web browser running HospitalRun, not a text editor or a mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client or API tool to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of terminal windows, database clients, or API testing tools being used to insert the patient data directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The patient to be registered must have the following exact details: First Name: 'Samuel', Last Name: 'Oduya', Sex: 'Male', Date of Birth: '1988-04-16', Blood Type: 'B+', Phone: '312-555-0147', and Address: '22 Harbor View Dr, Chicago, IL 60601'.
